title: podman requires dnsmasq for custom networks
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

I hit this in the Github actions CI, it seems that podman packages doesn't have dnsmasq as a required dependency.
Without dnsmasq custom networks fail.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

try to create a container with podman with a custom network without dnsmasq in the system

**Describe the results you received:**


> ERROR: failed to create cluster: podman run error: command "podman run --hostname kind-control-plane --name kind-control-plane --label io.x-k8s.kind.role=control-plane --privileged --tmpfs /tmp --tmpfs /run --volume 8e70bb2070bb8a9529ce05d177cad46f0717906498affb089d2f2d6857c63048:/var:suid,exec,dev --volume /lib/modules:/lib/modules:ro --detach --tty --net kind --label io.x-k8s.kind.cluster=kind -e container=podman --publish=127.0.0.1:41797:6443/tcp kindest/node@sha256:8f7ea6e7642c0da54f04a7ee10431549c0257315b3a634f6ef2fecaaedb19bab" failed with error: exit status 126
> 
> Command Output: WARNING: The same type, major and minor should not be used for multiple devices.
> time="2021-02-14T10:42:57Z" level=error msg="Error adding network: unable to locate dnsmasq in path"
> time="2021-02-14T10:42:57Z" level=error msg="Error while adding pod to CNI network \"kind\": unable to locate dnsmasq in path"
> 

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect that when I install podman from a package, it pulls all the required dependencies

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

xref issue in Github Actions https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/issues/2708



**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

The package is installed from the stable repo, 
https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/blob/875891b86b39572d2c947197c4222d4d62d38d39/images/linux/scripts/installers/containers.sh


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/issues/2708

comments.body:
The cni plugin dnsname requires the dnsmasq dependency. On fedora the dnsname plugin is install via the `podman-plugins` package which requires dnsmasq.
```
$ rpm -q podman-plugins --requires 
dnsmasq
...
```

> The cni plugin dnsname requires the dnsmasq dependency. On fedora the dnsname plugin is install via the `podman-plugins` package which requires dnsmasq.
> 
> ```
> $ rpm -q podman-plugins --requires 
> dnsmasq
> ...
> ```

so, what is the best solution for https://github.com/actions/virtual-environments/issues/2708?

You mention ubuntu 2004 so I assume it is installed via the kubic repo.

@lsm5 PTAL It looks like the `podman-plugins` debian package doesn't depend on `dnsmasq`

Should be fixed with podman 100:3.0.0-3. Building on OBS atm: https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/podman . Will be available hopefully in an hour or less.

I'll keep the bug open for now. Please close it if this resolves your issue.

Is it possible to depend on the `dnsmasq-base` package instead? that will get you the binary but skip the service and default config, `libvirt-daemon` debian packaging does the same thing. Or do you need a host level dnsmasq running specifically?

> Is it possible to depend on the `dnsmasq-base` package instead? that will get you the binary but skip the service and default config, `libvirt-daemon` debian packaging does the same thing. Or do you need a host level dnsmasq running specifically?

Agreed. Ubuntu is shipped with `systemd-resolved` enabled by default.
Introducing `dnsmasq` dependency only makes conflict as `dnsmasq` is not able to start.

> Is it possible to depend on the `dnsmasq-base` package instead? that will get you the binary but skip the service and default config, `libvirt-daemon` debian packaging does the same thing. Or do you need a host level dnsmasq running specifically?

I'll add the change, thanks

New podman-plugins building at: https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/podman-plugins 

New podman with updated podman-plugins dep ^ building at https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/podman

Should be available on the repos soon.
Closing, please reopen if you notice issues.
